page: Amazon Web Services c8id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The CPU architecture includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8). The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, providing high-speed local storage directly attached to the hypervisor. Networking capabilities feature a baseline bandwidth of 22 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance type.

In benchmark testing, the c8id.12xlarge demonstrates strong performance profiles. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2200.0 and a multi-core score of 19875.0. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads reaching 3859.38 GB/sec and large-block uncached reads at 194.95 GB/sec. The hardware-accelerated instruction sets contribute to strong cryptographic performance, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 54309.76, and top-tier text processing capabilities.

Given its compute-heavy profile, high-speed DDR5 memory, and local NVMe SSD storage, this server is optimized for workloads requiring high CPU throughput and fast local disk access. It is highly suitable for database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference, where it achieves 254.3 tokens/sec for 7B model prompt processing. The lack of a GPU limits its utility for massive parallel deep learning training, but the built-in Intel AMX extensions offer a cost-efficient alternative for CPU-based matrix operations and inference tasks without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. The CPU architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), which drive strong performance in cryptographic, text processing, and mathematical workloads. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance and strong memory read bandwidth. With a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps and no bundled GPU, this instance is highly efficient for CPU-intensive workloads, database operations, software compilation, and localized LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and compilation workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86 platform featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz with AMX and AVX-512 support.
Equipped with 96.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory.
Includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes.
Delivers 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
